summary:
The most common cause of post-neonatal mortality includes sepsis due to high incidences and lethality, with neonatal meningitis also playing a critical role through microbial infection risks, especially among premature babies. Environmental factors such as these infectious diseases are more strongly linked compared to genetic predisposition when examining primary contributors to this stage of infant mortality.
```

This summary captures the essential clinical points about the leading causes without unnecessary detail while addressing both sepsis and neonatal meningitis directly related to post-neonatal mortality. It emphasizes that environment-related issues—specifically infectious sources—are crucial determinants here over genetics.
```
